content.boundary: Applies to all villages under 2,000 inhabitants with a currently operating primary school where no new legal instrument is introduced; excludes villages that have already lost their school.
content.change_lever: No structural change; reliance on existing administrative discretion (temporary closure/suspension decisions) and ad hoc protest/political reversal channels as the sole checks on closure.
content.name: Status-quo continuation with informal ad hoc negotiation (passive-change counterfactual)
content.system_problem: Village school survival currently depends on discretionary, non-transparent closure decisions by tankerületi centers combined with unpredictable local political mobilization, producing inconsistent outcomes across similar villages and no institutionalized feedback mechanism linking demographic data to closure timing.
